---
title: python basics
---

```{r knitr_config, include=FALSE}
source("~/github/how2/_knitr_config.R")
```

*********************************************************************

## links

https://www.w3schools.com/python/default.asp  
https://www.w3schools.com/python/ref_string_format.asp  

*********************************************************************

## background

* created by Guido van Rossum
* released in 1991

*********************************************************************

## Installation

```{sh, eval=TRUE}
which python
python --version
```

```{sh, eval=TRUE}
conda activate py3
which python
python --version
```

```{r, eval=TRUE}
knitr::opts_chunk$get("engine.path")$python
```

*********************************************************************

## general

* Variable/function names are lower_case and separated_with_underscores  
* Named constants are in ALL_CAPITAL_LETTERS  
* Classes are in CamelCase  

*********************************************************************

## objects

### Variables

```{python, eval=TRUE}
x, y, z = "a", 1, 2.2
print(type(x))
print(type(y))
print(type(z))
```

#### str

Concatenate with `+`

```{python, eval=TRUE}
print("Orange" + "Banana")
```

Coerce/cast with `str()`

```{python, eval=TRUE}
print(str(x))
print(str(y))
print(str(z))
```

Create with `str()`

```{python, eval=TRUE}
print(str(1))
print(str(2.8))
print(str("3"))
```

Use `"""` or `'''` for multiline strings

```{python, eval=TRUE}
a = """Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet,
consectetur adipiscing elit,
sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt
ut labore et dolore magna aliqua."""
print(a)
```

Index with `[`

```{python, eval=TRUE}
a = "Hello, World!"
print(a[0])
print(a[0:5])
print(a[-1])
print(a[-6:])
```

Built-in functions

```{python, eval=TRUE}
a = "    Hello, World!    "
print(len(a))
```

Methods

```{python, eval=TRUE}
a = "    Hello, World!    "
print(a.lower())
print(a.upper())
print(a.strip())
print(a.replace("H", "J"))
print(a.split(","))
```

```{python, eval=TRUE}
name = "Darren"
age = "41"
txt = "Hello, my name is {} and I am {}"
txt.format(name, age)
```

#### int

Coerce/cast with `int()`

```{python, eval=TRUE}
# print(int(x)) # throws and error
print(int(y))
print(int(z))
```

Create with `int()`

```{python, eval=TRUE}
print(int(1))
print(int(2.8))
print(int("3"))
```

#### float

Coerce/cast with `float()`

```{python, eval=TRUE}
# print(float(x)) # throws and error
print(float(y))
print(float(z))
```

Create with `float()`

```{python, eval=TRUE}
print(float(1))
print(float(2.8))
print(float("3"))
```

### collections

property                      | list  | tuple | set   | dict
:---------------------------- | ----- | ----- | ----- | -----
ordered                       | yes   | yes   |       | 
changeable                    | yes   |       | yes   | yes 
indexed                       | yes   | yes   |       | yes    
duplicates allowed            | yes   | yes   |       |     
constructor                   | `[]`  | `()`  | `{}`  | `{}`
                              |       |       |       | 
**method**                    | **list** | **tuple** | **set** | **dict**
`add`                         |       |       | yes   | 
`append`                      |  yes  |       |       |  
`clear`                       |  yes  |       | yes   | yes 
`copy`                        |  yes  |       | yes   | yes 
`count`                       |  yes  |  yes  |       |  
`difference`                  |       |       | yes   | 
`difference_update`           |       |       | yes   | 
`discard`                     |       |       | yes   | 
`extend`                      |  yes  |       |       |  
`fromkeys`                    |       |       |       | yes
`get`                         |       |       |       | yes
`index`                       |  yes  |  yes  |       |  
`insert`                      |  yes  |       |       |  
`intersection`                |       |       | yes   | 
`intersection_update`         |       |       | yes   | 
`isdispoint`                  |       |       | yes   | 
`issubset`                    |       |       | yes   | 
`issuperset`                  |       |       | yes   | 
`items`                       |       |       |       | yes
`keys`                        |       |       |       | yes
`pop`                         |  yes  |       | yes   | yes 
`popitems`                    |       |       |       | yes
`remove`                      |  yes  |       | yes   |  
`reverse`                     |  yes  |       |       |  
`setdefault`                  |       |       |       | yes
`sort`                        |  yes  |       |       |  
`symmetric_difference`        |       |       | yes   | 
`symmetric_difference_update` |       |       | yes   | 
`union`                       |       |       | yes   | 
`update`                      |       |       | yes   | yes
`values`                      |       |       |       | yes


Constructors:

```{python, eval=TRUE}
l = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]
t = ("apple", "banana", "cherry")
s = {"apple", "banana", "cherry"}
d = {"apple" : "green", "banana" : "yellow", "cherry" : "red"}
```

Alternative constructors:

```{python, eval=TRUE}
l = list(("apple", "banana", "cherry"))
t = tuple(("apple", "banana", "cherry"))
s = set(("apple", "banana", "cherry"))
d = dict(apple = "green", banana = "yellow", cherry = "red")
```

Class:

```{python, eval=TRUE}
type(l)
type(t)
type(s)
type(d)
```

Built-in functions:

```{python, eval=TRUE}
len(l)
len(t)
len(s)
len(d)
```

Indexing:

```{python, eval=TRUE}
l[0]
t[0]
# s[0] # throws an error
# d[0] # throws an error
d["apple"]
```

Loops:

```{python, eval=TRUE}
for element in l: print(element)
for element in t: print(element)
for element in s: print(element)
for key in d: print(key) # print keys
for key in d: print(d[key]) # print values
for value in d.values(): print(value)  # print values
for key, value in d.items(): print(key, value) # print keys and values
```

Querying:
  
```{python, eval=TRUE}
if "banana" in l: print("I love bananas")
if "banana" in t: print("I love bananas")
if "banana" in s: print("I love bananas")
if "banana" in d: print("I love bananas")
```

Set methods:

```{python, eval=TRUE}
s.add("orange")
s.update(["mango", "grapes"])
print(s)
s.discard("pear") # no error
# s.remove("pear") # error
```

Dict methods:

```{python, eval=TRUE}
d["apple"]
d.get("model")
d["apple"] = "red"
d["pear"] = "green"
print(d)
d.popitem()
print(d)
```

Keywords:

```{python, eval=TRUE}
del l[0]
print(l)
# del t[0] # throws an error
# del s[0] # throws an error
# del d[0] # throws an error
#del d["cherry"]
#print(d)
```

```{python, eval=TRUE}
del l
del t
del s
del d
```

*********************************************************************

## operators

**Arithmetic**

`%` - modulus  
`**` - exponentiation  
`//` - floor division  

**Logical**

`x < 5 and x < 10`  
`x < 5 or x < 4`  
`not(x < 5 and x < 10)`  

**Identity**

`x is y`  
`x is not y`  

**Membership**

`x in y`  
`x not in y`  

**Bitwise**

`&` : binary AND  
`|` : binary OR  
`^` : binary XOR  
`~` : binary flip bits  
`<<` : binary left shift  
`>>` : binary right shift  

```{python, eval=TRUE}
a = 60        #  60 = 0011 1100 
b = 13        #  13 = 0000 1101 
print(a & b)  #  12 = 0000 1100 
print(a | b)  #  61 = 0011 1101
print(a ^ b)  #  49 = 0011 0001
print(~a)     # -61 = 1100 0011
print(a << 2) # 240 = 1111 0000
print(a >> 2) #  15 = 0000 1111
```

**Assignment**

`x += 3` is the same as `x = x + 3`	 
`x -= 3` is the same as `x = x - 3`  
`x *= 3` is the same as `x = x * 3`	 
`x /= 3` is the same as `x = x / 3`	 
`x %= 3` is the same as `x = x % 3`	 
`x //= 3` is the same as `x = x // 3`	 
`x **= 3` is the same as `x = x ** 3`	 
`x ^= 3` is the same as `x = x ^ 3`  
`x &= 3` is the same as `x = x & 3`	 
`x |= 3` is the same as `x = x | 3`	 

*********************************************************************

## functions

Loops: range (X), 
Iterators
Error handling
Scripting - passing args via command line
Modules - write your own
Modules built in
datetime
json
Sys
Os
Re
File paths
Database connections
Boto3

*********************************************************************

## dates and times

*********************************************************************

## file paths

```python
sys.path.append('{}/myproject/'.format(os.path.expanduser('~')))
from mysript import myfunction
```

*********************************************************************

## databases

*********************************************************************

## docstrings

```python
def square(value):
    """Return the square of a value"""
    new_value = value ** 2
    return new_value
```

*********************************************************************

## scope

* global - defines the main body of a script  
* local - defined inside a function  
* built-in - names in the pre-defined built-ins module  

### nonlocal

* used when defining a function that is nested inside another function
* allows the nested function to use/modify variables defined in the outer function

```{python}
def myfunc1():
    x = "John"
    def myfunc2():
        nonlocal x
        x = "hello"
    myfunc2() 
    return x

print(myfunc1())
```

*********************************************************************

## unpacking 

e.g. tuples

```python
even_nums = (2, 4, 6)
a, b, c = even_nums 
```

*********************************************************************

Attribute
Data that is associated with an instance
Accessed using object.attribute
A variable
Can have class attributes or instance attributes
accessed in the same way via a ‘dot’
If same name used for both class and instance attribute then instance attribute is accessed first = attribute lookup order
print dir(x)



Augmented assignment
E.g. replace x = x + 1, with x += 1

Class
A blueprint for an instance - an ‘instance factory’
Class constructor functions conventionally start with a capital letter
Classes are also objects

Decorators
A processor that modifies a function
Start with @
E.g for modifying the default binding that instance methods provide: 
@classmethod takes the class as input not the instance
@staticmethod requires no args and does not work with the class or the instance

Encapsulation
One of the three pillars of OOP
Refers to the safe storage of data (as attributes) in an instance
E.g. setter methods to safely set/modify instance attributes

Getter method
A method that gets attribute values from the instance

Inheritance
One of the three pillars of OOP

__init__
Special constructor method that allows us to initialise attributes at the time an instance is constructed
Called automatically whenever a new instance is constructed 
Optional - you don’t have to use it if you don’t need it
(an example of a ‘magic’ function)
(Underscores signify private method, intended for use internally)

Instance
a constructed object of a given class

__main__ 
refers to the namespace of the script currently being executed

Method
a callable attribute defined in the class
allows you to change the state of the instance
A method on an instance always implicitly passes the instance as the first argument to the method
When the function is defined in the class, the instance argument is represented by ‘self’
‘self’ is the instance upon which the method was called
Instance methods are known as ‘bound’ methods- i.e. they are bound to the instance upon which they are called

Method resolution order (MRO)
The order of lookup when an object inherits from multiple classes
Depth first

None
When a function doesn’t return anything it actually returns the None value

Object
a unit of data (i.e. attributes) 
of a particular type (i.e. an instance of a class) 
with associated functionality (i.e. methods)

Object-Oriented Programming (OOP)
A paradigm for code organization and design
Data organised into objects 
Functionality organised into methods
Helps manage complexity
Primary paradigm for software design

Polymorphism
One of the three pillars of OOP
Use the same interface for different classes - e.g. use len() on a string or a list.
E.g. call dir(obj) and you may see things like __len__, which means you can access the obj.__len__() method via the built-in function len - i.e. len(obj)

Setter method
A method that sets attribute values of the instance

State
the values of the attributes of an instance

Type
indicates the class the instance belongs to
